name: Watchwire Energy Intelligence API
what_it_does: Watchwire Energy Intelligence REST API for enterprise energy data management, utility analytics, and sustainability reporting. Enables AI agents to manage automated utility bill capture and interval data for energy expense management, handle energy benchmark and performance tracking across facility portfolio for sustainability analytics, access carbon footprint calculation and GHG inventory reporting for emissions compliance, retrieve energy budget and cost variance analysis for expense management, manage renewable energy and sustainability certificate tracking for clean energy portfolio, handle demand response and time-of-use rate optimization for energy cost reduction, access energy efficiency project measurement and verification for ROI tracking, retrieve building energy performance and ENERGY STAR score tracking, manage energy procurement and contract optimization for utility rate management, and integrate Watchwire with BAS, IoT, ERP, and ESG reporting platforms.
use_cases: Automating utility data capture from energy management agents
Tracking carbon emissions from ESG reporting agents
Benchmarking building energy from sustainability agents
Optimizing energy costs from procurement agents
not_for: Small businesses without multi-facility energy management
Real-time grid balancing without utility bill focus
Consumer energy management without enterprise context
best_when: A large corporate, real estate portfolio, university, or government wanting AI agents to automate utility data capture, energy benchmarking, GHG emissions tracking, sustainability reporting, and energy cost optimization.
avoid_when: GHG REPORTING ACCURACY: Automated GHG Protocol Scope 1/2/3 calculations for regulatory reporting (SEC proposed climate rules, California AB 1305, CSRD) must use verified emission factors and comply with GHG Protocol accounting standards; automated emissions calculations with incorrect methodology create material ESG disclosure risk. Utility data quality — automated utility bill capture via EDI or utility API may have data quality issues (missing bills, incorrect billing periods, duplicate invoices); automated energy analytics without data quality validation creates reporting inaccuracies. REC and carbon offset greenwashing — automated renewable energy claims must verify REC retirement in certified registries (WREGIS, NEPOOL GIS, M-RETS); unverified automated clean energy claims violate FTC Green Guides and create greenwashing liability.

auth.notes: Watchwire uses API key and OAuth 2.0. REST API with JSON. Chicago, Illinois HQ. Founded 2001. Private (Generac Power Systems acquired 2021). 500+ enterprise and institutional customers. Energy intelligence platform focused on corporate real estate and sustainability. Competes with EnergyCAP, Urjanet, and Schneider Electric for utility bill management and energy analytics.

pricing.free_tier_limits: No free tier — per-facility or enterprise subscription
pricing.paid_tiers: Annual subscription; per-meter or per-building pricing
pricing.notes: Chicago, Illinois. Generac Power subsidiary (acquired 2021). Founded 2001. 500+ enterprise customers. Energy intelligence and sustainability. Competes with EnergyCAP and Schneider for energy management.

performance.rate_limits: Rate limits per API key; bulk data export endpoints available

known_agent_gotchas: GHG PROTOCOL METHODOLOGY COMPLIANCE: Automated GHG Scope 1/2/3 calculations must use GHG Protocol-compliant methodology and updated emission factors (EPA eGRID, IEA regional factors); incorrect methodology or stale emission factors create material sustainability disclosure inaccuracy

Utility data completeness validation — automated utility data capture via EDI may have coverage gaps (missing accounts, late bills, data format errors); automated energy reporting without completeness validation creates portfolio coverage gaps in sustainability reporting
No webhooks — energy data and sustainability event notification requires polling; no native push for new utility data availability or budget variance threshold breach

REC registry verification for clean energy claims — automated renewable energy certificate tracking must verify REC retirement in certified registries; automated RECs purchased but not retired create invalid clean energy claims
Generac acquisition integration — Watchwire acquired by Generac in 2021; post-acquisition product direction and API investment should be monitored for long-term platform stability

Demand response data requirements — automated demand response and TOU optimization requires near-real-time interval meter data; utility EDI data may lag 24-48 hours and is unsuitable for real-time demand response automation
agent_readiness.security_notes: Energy intelligence. SOC2. API key/OAuth2. US. Utility and energy consumption data.

security_score_components.security_notes: Energy intelligence. SOC2. API key/OAuth2. US. Utility and energy data.
